svg.text: 38-Preset Points
I. What is a Preset Point?

A Preset Point is a data record of location and orientation within a Scene. Preset Points can be referenced by features that require points data, such as Spawn Points, Revive Points, and Node Graph retrieval points.
II. Editing Preset Points
Preset Point Library
All Preset Points are listed in the library window. Visibility of each point can be toggled in the Layout Scene.
Persistent Display Toggle
When on: all Preset Points remain visible even if the management tool is disabled. When off: points become invisible when the tool is disabled.
Creating a Preset Point
Click "Create Preset Point" to generate a new Preset Point at the center of the current Edit Window, with naming editing mode enabled automatically.
Preset Point Parameters
Preset Point Name: customizable name for the point.
Preset Point Index: unique identifier used as an input parameter for Nodes.
Lock Transform: when enabled, position and rotation cannot be adjusted.
Display in Scene: when unchecked, only coordinate axes remain visible.
Location and Rotation: sets the position and orientation data of the Preset Point.
III. Referencing Preset Points
Spawn Points
In Stage Settings, configure Spawn Point by selecting a Preset Point as the spawn location.
Revive Points
In Stage Settings, configure Revive Point by selecting a Preset Point as the revive location.
Node Graph
Query Preset Point Position Rotation by Index, or get a Preset Point List filtered by Unit Tag.
AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
